pearl_key: antiphospholipid antibody laboratory testing indicates whether antibodies are present that recognize clotting proteins not that they actually have the special property that enables them to activate the clotting cascade thus false positive testing is a challenge in particular transient false positives are common in the setting of acute inflammation which is why diagnostic criteria require that testing is persistently positive for at least 12 weeks
pearl: Antiphospholipid antibody laboratory testing indicates whether antibodies are present that recognize clotting proteins, not that they actually have the special property that enables them to activate the clotting cascade. In particular, transient false positives are common in the setting of acute inflammation, which is why diagnostic criteria require that testing is persistently positive for at least 12 weeks.

pearl_key: a non contrast ct scan allows assessment of the density of the adrenal mass which provides information about whether the mass is lipid rich 10 hounsfield units or lipid poor 10 hounsfield units lipid rich masses are most likely benign adenomas and are highly unlikely to be cancer or pheochromocytomas while lipid poor masses may be a primary adrenal malignancy metastatic cancer pheochromocytoma or a lipid poor benign adenoma
pearl: A non-contrast CT scan allows assessment of the density of the adrenal mass, which provides information about whether the mass is lipid-rich (<10 Hounsfield units) or lipid-poor (>10 Hounsfield units.) Lipid-rich masses are most likely benign adenomas and are highly unlikely to be cancer or pheochromocytomas, while lipid-poor masses may be a primary adrenal malignancy, metastatic cancer, pheochromocytoma, or a lipid-poor benign adenoma.

links.rationale: Young 2007 supports non-contrast CT attenuation distinguishing lipid-rich from lipid-poor masses.
Corwin 2022 discusses limited utility of contrast wash-out, informing which imaging features matter.

pearl_key: dr young s expert opinion is that all patients with a lipid rich mass should receive one follow up scan at 1 year patients with clearly lipid poor masses e g unenhanced ct attenuation 20 hounsfield units should undergo surgical intervention or close monitoring with indefinite follow up
pearl: Dr. Young’s expert opinion is that all patients with a lipid-rich mass should receive one follow-up scan at 1 year. Patients with clearly lipid-poor masses (e.g. unenhanced CT attenuation >20 Hounsfield units) should undergo surgical intervention or close monitoring with indefinite follow-up.

links.rationale: Iniguez-Ariza 2017 supports that imaging phenotype over size guides surgical decisions.

pearl_key: all patients with adrenal incidentalomas should be screened for subclinical cushing s syndrome with acth dheas and cortisol levels if dheas is 100 mcg dl this suggests that the patient does not have subclinical cushing s and the evaluation can stop while if dheas is 100 mcg dl the evaluation should proceed with a 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test dr young also recommends that all patients with a positive 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test undergo a confirmatory 8 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test prior to surgical intervention to avoid false positives
pearl: All patients with adrenal incidentalomas should be screened for subclinical Cushing’s syndrome with ACTH, DHEAS, and cortisol levels. If DHEAS is >100 mcg/dL, this suggests that the patient does not have subclinical Cushing’s and the evaluation can stop, while if DHEAS is <100 mcg/dL, the evaluation should proceed with a 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test. Dr. Young also recommends that all patients with a positive 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test undergo a confirmatory 8 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test prior to surgical intervention, to avoid false positives.

links.rationale: Carafone 2021 supports DHEAS use in evaluating subclinical Cushing's.
Dennedy 2017 supports DHEAS as biomarker to decide whether further Cushing's testing is needed.
Chiodini 2009 provides rationale for why evaluating subclinical Cushing's matters.
Kebebew 2021 describes subclinical Cushing's clinical features.

links.rationale: Canu 2019 supports pheochromocytoma screening when attenuation >10 HU and not for lipid-rich.
